text:
American gymnast Simone Biles is a busy woman.

A little over two weeks after she won gold in the women's individual all-around final at the Summer Olympics in Paris, Biles was back on American soil cheering on her husbandâChicago Bearssafety Jonathan Owens. Owens recorded three tackles in the Bears' 27â3 exhibition win over theCincinnati BengalsSaturday.

In a MondayTikTok, however, Biles revealed that she will likely attend few Bears games this seasonâbecause of her post-Olympic obligations.

"I don't know how many games I'll be able to make because in a few weeks we go on tour, and if you guys aren't aware, it's the Gold Over America tour. I think right now we're doing about 32 stops," Biles said.

The tour, a traveling gymnastics exhibition featuring Biles and a spate of other gymnasts, is scheduled to commence Sept. 16 in Oceanside, Calif.

Chicago opens its season eight days prior against theTennessee Titans.

Patrick Andres is a staff writer on the Breaking and Trending News team at Sports Illustrated. He joined SI in December 2022, having worked for The Blade, Athlon Sports, Fear the Sword and Diamond Digest. Andres has covered everything from zero-attendance Big Ten basketball to a seven-overtime college football game. He is a graduate of Northwestern University's Medill School of Journalism with a double major in history .

title: Simone Biles Explains Why She Wonât Be Able to Attend Many Bears Games This Season
description: The gymnast has a number of obligations to meet.
